---
title: "SEC_R003: GitIgnore Security Requirements"
subtitle: "Mandatory .gitignore entries for credential protection"
chapter: "CH07"
category: "rule"
number: "SEC_R003"
date-created: "2025-08-30"
date-modified: "2025-08-30"
author: "Principle Revisor"
type: "security-rule"
parent-principle: "MP110"
enforcement: "REQUIRED"
severity: "HIGH"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
---

## Rule Statement

Every repository MUST have a properly configured .gitignore file that prevents credentials, secrets, and sensitive configuration files from being committed to version control.

## Mandatory .gitignore Entries

### Core Security Entries

```gitignore
# Environment Files - CRITICAL
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
!.env.template
*.env
env/

# API Keys and Secrets
*_api_key*
*_secret*
*_token*
*_password*
credentials/
secrets/
*.key
*.pem
*.p12
*.pfx

# Configuration with Secrets
config/production.yml
config/staging.yml
database.yml
secrets.yml
*_credentials.json

# Cloud Provider Credentials
.aws/
.gcp/
.azure/
*.serviceaccount.json
```

### Platform-Specific Entries

```gitignore
# R/RStudio
.Rprofile
.Renviron
.httr-oauth
*.Rproj.user

# Python
*.pyc
__pycache__/
instance/
.python-version

# Node.js
node_modules/
.npmrc

# Database
*.sqlite
*.db
dump.sql
```

### Application-Specific

```gitignore
# Shiny Apps
rsconnect/
.secrets/
app_secrets.R

# Jupyter
.ipynb_checkpoints/
*.ipynb_credentials

# IDE
.vscode/settings.json
.idea/workspace.xml
```

## Implementation Template

### Complete .gitignore Template

```gitignore
# =========================
# SECURITY - NEVER REMOVE
# =========================

# Environment Variables
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
!.env.template
env/
*.env

# Credentials and Secrets
*api_key*
*secret*
*token*
*password*
*credential*
secrets/
credentials/
private/

# Certificates and Keys
*.key
*.pem
*.p12
*.pfx
*.crt
*.cer
ssl/

# =========================
# PLATFORM SPECIFIC
# =========================

# R/RStudio
.Rhistory
.Rprofile
.Renviron
.httr-oauth
*.Rproj.user
.Rproj.user/

# Python
*.pyc
__pycache__/
venv/
.env/
instance/

# =========================
# APPLICATION
# =========================

# Shiny
rsconnect/
app_cache/
www/temp/

# Data (if sensitive)
data/*.csv
data/*.xlsx
!data/example.csv

# Logs
*.log
logs/

# =========================
# OS FILES
# =========================

# macOS
.DS_Store
.AppleDouble
.LSOverride

# Windows
Thumbs.db
ehthumbs.db
Desktop.ini

# Linux
*~
.directory
```

## Validation Script

### Check .gitignore Completeness

```r
# Function to validate .gitignore
validate_gitignore <- function(gitignore_path = ".gitignore") {
  required_patterns <- c(
    ".env",
    "*.key",
    "*.pem",
    "*secret*",
    "*password*",
    "*credential*",
    ".Renviron",
    ".Rprofile"
  )
  
  if (!file.exists(gitignore_path)) {
    stop(".gitignore file not found!")
  }
  
  gitignore_content <- readLines(gitignore_path)
  
  missing_patterns <- character()
  for (pattern in required_patterns) {
    if (!any(grepl(pattern, gitignore_content, fixed = TRUE))) {
      missing_patterns <- c(missing_patterns, pattern)
    }
  }
  
  if (length(missing_patterns) > 0) {
    warning(sprintf(
      "Missing security patterns in .gitignore: %s",
      paste(missing_patterns, collapse = ", ")
    ))
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  message("✓ .gitignore properly configured for security")
  return(TRUE)
}

# Run validation
validate_gitignore()
```

## Enforcement

### Pre-commit Hook

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for required .gitignore entries
required_patterns=(
  ".env"
  "*.key"
  "*secret*"
  "*password*"
)

for pattern in "${required_patterns[@]}"; do
  if ! grep -q "$pattern" .gitignore; then
    echo "ERROR: .gitignore missing required pattern: $pattern"
    exit 1
  fi
done

# Check for accidental credential commits
if git diff --cached --name-only | xargs grep -E "(password|secret|token|key)\s*=" 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: Possible credential detected in staged files"
  exit 1
fi
```

## Recovery Procedures

### If Sensitive File Was Committed

```bash
# Remove file from repository history
git filter-branch --force --index-filter \
  "git rm --cached --ignore-unmatch path/to/sensitive/file" \
  --prune-empty --tag-name-filter cat -- --all

# Force push (coordinate with team)
git push --force --all
git push --force --tags

# Clean up local repository
git for-each-ref --format='delete %(refname)' refs/original | git update-ref --stdin
git reflog expire --expire=now --all
git gc --prune=now
```

## Audit Checklist

- [ ] .gitignore exists in repository root
- [ ] All environment file patterns included
- [ ] All credential patterns included
- [ ] Platform-specific patterns added
- [ ] No sensitive files in git history
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks configured
- [ ] Team trained on .gitignore importance

## Related Rules

- SEC_R001: No Hardcoded Credentials
- SEC_R002: Environment Variable Standards
- SEC_R004: Secret Detection Tools
